creator: raimeetravel
caption:
Americans NEED to take these steps before going abroad!👇

👋 I’m @raimeetravel and I help you travel smarter with actionable, detailed tips & itineraries! I have a 9-5 job but travel the world whenever I can and show you how to do the same 🫶🏻

I got the inside scoop at the @statedept Travel Creator Open House on how to stay safe, avoid scams, and travel smarter!! Here’s what you need to know:
 💬Comment LIST and I’ll send you my pre-trip checklist with ALL the links & tips! 

1️⃣ Entry Requirements: Always check passport validity, visas, or authorization forms (like the new one for Europe starting next year). Don’t skip this step! ✈️ 

I recently assumed I’d be fine to enter New Zealand without anything but turns out I needed an authorization form and I could have been denied entry!!! 😅

2️⃣ Safety Info: On Travel.State.Gov (@travelgov), you’ll find:
* Safety levels by region (some areas might be safer than others).
* Maps & tips to avoid common scams (did you know about the dating app scam in Colombia?).
* Even info on roads/highways to avoid. 🚧

3️⃣ Local Laws: Did you know you’re subject to the laws of the country you’re visiting? Many Americans get detained abroad over laws they didn’t know about … including things like carrying medications that are totally legal here BUT might not be abroad! Read up to avoid mistakes! 🛑

4️⃣ Pro Tip: Enroll in STEP (Smart Traveler Enrollment Program) for real-time alerts on protests, safety concerns, and new laws in your destination. It’s like a safety lifeline while you travel! 🛡️

Other must-knows: 📍 You can renew your passport online now! 📍 Travel.State.Gov has resources for emergency passports! 📍 Always get travel insurance—your credit card may not cover enough!

Do you have any other questions about staying safe, visas, or travel prep? I learned a lot and can answer some below!

Also shout out to all the AMAZING travel creators I met here 🥹 @kevindroniak @journeyswithkris @ncolphillip @maxmilespoints @zacharyburrabel @paigesecosky @cici.inthesky 
 Comment LIST and I’ll send you my pre-trip checklist with ALL the links & tips! 🌍✨
.

transcription.text: Not to scare you, but there are a lot of things American travelers should be doing before they go abroad that they are just not doing. So I'm going somewhere very cool today to learn about these pre-trip musts straight from the source. These are essential to help you stay safe, avoid scams, and just travel smarter. I was invited by the US State Department to learn about this information. So here's what you need to know before your next international trip. Number one, head to travel.state.gov. Type in the country you're going to visit and check the entry requirements. You might need certain passport validity or certain visas or authorization forms. Like next year, you'll need one to enter Europe. Second, do a deep dive into the safety section. While a country in general might be rated a level two, certain areas in that country might be ranked higher or lower. And you'll be able to see color-coded detailed maps about where to avoid. Plus, this safety and security area will tell you about common scams to watch out for, like a current dating app scam that's happening in Colombia right now, and even which specific highways and roads to avoid. Third, don't forget to look at the local laws section. Too many Americans wind up being detained because of silly mistakes they just weren't aware of that were breaking laws. Number four, a pro tip. Sign up for Step. This is the Smart Traveler Enrollment Program. Step will send you real-time alerts about things happening in the countries you're visiting, like protests, demonstrations, or new laws that could affect your plans. Think of it as your travel safety lifeline. And finally, here's some rapid-fire quick tips. As of September, you can now renew your passport online. Plus, there's information about how to get an emergency passport if you need one fast. Don't pay for one of those rush services. And please make sure to get travel insurance. We heard a lot of horror stories of Americans who ended up spending thousands of dollars because they didn't have it. Comment list below, and I'll send you my pre-trip checklist with all of these must-know resources and more.
